title: JTBD — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation (Standalone BESS / IPP)

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape (reused from VN canvas)
sections.content:
### 1.1 CFJ + Output Types

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| NAICS | 221118 Other Electric Power Generation |
| CFJ | Deliver dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy on demand to the power system, earning revenue from flexibility, capacity, and ancillary-service markets |
| Output types | Standalone BESS · PV+BESS hybrid · Wind+BESS hybrid · DSO Netzbooster · C&I behind-the-meter · HPC buffer |
| Industry offering level | L7 ecosystem (IPP sells dispatched electricity + grid services) |

### 1.2 Segments (beneficiary = IPP organization type)

| # | Segment | Circumstance |
|---|---|---|
| S1 | Pure-play BESS IPP | Fund- or developer-backed, greenfield projects, merchant or tolled revenue |
| S2 | Utility-owned IPP arm | RWE, EnBW, Vattenfall subsidiaries; procurement-heavy, regulated risk appetite |
| S3 | Solar/wind developer extending to storage | Brownfield retrofit onto operational renewables park; hybrid revenue |
| S4 | Infrastructure fund / financial sponsor | Passive equity; outsources everything to owner's engineer + EPC |
| S5 | Municipal Stadtwerke / coop | Small-scale (5–50 MWh), local grid-service focus, public procurement |
| S6 | Aggregator-led asset ownership | Entrix / Next Kraftwerke anchor tolling, finance via SPV |

### 1.3 Process VN (summary — full detail in VN ref file)

L6a Site Development · L6b Engineering · L6c Procurement · **L6d Construction & Integration** · L6e Commissioning & Cert · L6f Operations · L6g Decommissioning. Horizontal: HSE, QA, Cyber, Regulatory.

### 1.4 / 1.5 BOM + Edges

Not derived — service/ecosystem NAICS. Service composition is documented via L5/L4 in VN file.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Route | **PROCESS_VN** — EPC service creates the asset; does not become part of a consumable output |
| Anchor VN unit | Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services |
| Anchor level | L5 |
| Parent VN unit | Construction & Integration |
| Parent level | L6 |
| Job-context implication | Product exists to serve L6d's need: **convert a greenlit storage business case into a commissioned, grid-compliant, revenue-generating asset** |

### 2.2 Lens

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Function verb (from L6d job_family) | *Integrate & commission* |
| Beneficiary (primary) | IPP / asset-owner company |
| Lens statement | "What integration-and-commissioning execution does an IPP need when converting a storage investment thesis into a running asset?" |

### 2.3 Core Job + Product Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Core Job (customer phrasing) | "Turn my greenlit storage business case into a safe, certified, revenue-generating asset on schedule, on budget, and without me having to integrate it myself" |
| Product Job statement | "Help Construction & Integration to deliver a commissioned, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8-certified multi-MWh BESS asset within contracted COD, CAPEX, and performance guarantees under single-source accountability" |
| Abstraction test | PASS — ≥3 alternatives from different categories (OEM turnkey, pure-play EPC, split-contract, defer) |

### 2.4 Alternatives (5 per Core Job)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC (neutral) | Category | Inherent trade-offs | Existential? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Solar-EPC extension into BESS** (Mein Solar's category: BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck) | 72151500 | Same category | Strong grid-code / civil depth, weaker cell & EMS OEM leverage; thin BESS reference count vs. OEM giants | no |
| A2 | **OEM-led turnkey** (Fluence, Tesla Megapack, Sungrow PowerTitan, Wärtsilä) | 72151500 | Different category | Equipment-bundled, deep cell/PCS integration; vendor lock-in, weaker local civil + permit execution, less PV-hybrid flexibility | no |
| A3 | **Pure-play BESS EPC integrator** (Kyon Energy, GreenTech Energy, SolarEast EPC) | 72151500 | Different category | Storage-first pedigree, OEM-agnostic procurement; smaller balance sheet, no PV-hybrid history | no |
| A4 | **Split-contract / owner-managed multi-prime** (DNV/Fichtner PMC + separate lots) | 81101513 | Different approach | Lowest price ceiling, maximum OEM choice; owner carries all interface + schedule risk | no |
| A5 | **Defer or cancel project** (wait for cells to drop, policy clarity) | — | Status quo | Zero CAPEX + zero integration burden; forgoes revenue window + permits + grid-capacity slot (226 GW DE queue) | **yes** |

### 2.5 Scenarios / Output Type Activation

| Scenario | Activated output type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 10–50 MWh standalone merchant BESS | Standalone BESS IPP | Mein Solar's size class — reference fit |
| PV-park retrofit (20–40 MWh BESS on operational PV) | PV+BESS hybrid | Differentiator-activating; PV-EPC heritage applies |
| 100+ MWh standalone merchant | Standalone BESS IPP | Above Mein Solar's 9 MWh reference; syndication required |
| DSO Netzbooster procurement | Netzbooster | Regulated utility customer (S2); different motion |
| HPC buffer for charging hub | HPC buffer | FP-extension scenario; separate JTBD file |

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### 3.1 Burleson Pyramid (per stakeholder role)

Stakeholders present (per Ulwick B2B deep service):

| Role | Instance | Active levels |
|---|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | IPP sponsor / asset-owner company | P2, P3, P4, P5 |
| Job Executor | Owner's project director (customer-side PM) | P1 (all 5), P2 |
| Job Overseer | Investment committee / CFO | P2, P4 |
| Product Lifecycle Support | O&M provider (post-COD), DSO interface engineer, insurance underwriter | P1 own stage |
| Purchase Executor | CFO / Procurement Director | P1 Acquisition |
| Purchase Influencer | Owner's Engineer (DNV, Fichtner), Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier | P4 |

### 3.2 Job Map — Ulwick 8 steps × relevance

| # | Step | Relevant? | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | ✅ | EPC fixes scope, grid-code target, revenue-stack enablement, performance SLA |
| 2 | LOCATE | ✅ | EPC runs grid-capacity study + Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung + equipment sourcing |
| 3 | PREPARE | ✅ | EPC executes permitting, civil works, protection study, site logistics |
| 4 | CONFIRM | ✅ | FAT, SAT, protection-relay setting, trip tests, pre-cert readiness |
| 5 | EXECUTE | ✅ | Container placement, MV/LV install, energization, grid synchronization |
| 6 | MONITOR | ✅ | Schedule, budget, HSE, Brandschutz, quality during the build |
| 7 | MODIFY | ✅ | Change orders, grid-code adjustments, DSO-acceptance rework |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | ✅ | FGW TR8 cert, DSO acceptance, handover dossier, performance guarantee bond |

All 8 relevant → need count target: **22–28 error statements**.

### 3.3 Error Statements (22 needs × 7 error types × P1/P2/P3-P5)

| # | Step | Error type | Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | Prod-rel | Pyramid |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of CAPEX-vs-actual deviation exceeding ±5% between EPC offer and final commissioned cost | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | P2 |
| 2 | DEFINE | EFFORT | Minimize the effort for the IPP sponsor to translate a business-case spreadsheet into a buildable, grid-code-compliant technical specification (<4 weeks) | 8 | 5 | 11 | time | false | P1-Acq |
| 3 | DEFINE | CONSISTENCY | Minimize the likelihood that more than 1 interface (battery / PCS / MV / EMS) carries ambiguous responsibility between EPC and supplier scopes | 9 | 3 | 15 | reliability | true (A2/A4-caused) | P2 |
| 4 | LOCATE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of an undetected DSO capacity shortfall surfacing after FID (reducing delivered capacity >20%) | 10 | 4 | 16 | cost | false | P2 |
| 5 | LOCATE | THROUGHPUT | Maximize the rate of equipment-lead-time parallelization so that critical-path procurement (cells, PCS, transformer) stays <10 months | 8 | 5 | 11 | time | false | P2 |
| 6 | LOCATE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of a single-vendor cell-supply concentration exposing >70% of project CAPEX to one country-of-origin tariff / CRMA risk | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | false | P2 |
| 7 | PREPARE | EFFORT | Minimize the number of separate Baugenehmigung, Brandschutzkonzept, and BImSchG submissions the IPP sponsor has to track (≤1 via EPC wrapper) | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | true (A4-caused) | P1-Prep |
| 8 | PREPARE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection design rework (additional walls, separation, hydrant runs) after local Feuerwehr review | 8 | 4 | 12 | safety | false | P2 |
| 9 | PREPARE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of civil rework (foundation, trenching) caused by late geotechnical surprises exceeding 5% of civil budget | 6 | 5 | 7 | cost | false | P2 |
| 10 | CONFIRM | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of a FAT/SAT protection-relay setting failing first trip test on energization day | 9 | 5 | 13 | reliability | false | P2 |
| 11 | CONFIRM | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of an undetected unit-certificate (FGW TR3) documentation gap delaying plant-cert (TR8) submission >4 weeks | 9 | 3 | 15 | time | false | P2 |
| 12 | EXECUTE | THROUGHPUT | Maximize the likelihood of grid-synchronization within the contracted COD window (slip ≤30 days) | 10 | 4 | 16 | cost | false | P2 |
| 13 | EXECUTE | RECOVERY | Minimize the time to recover from a failed commissioning test without re-triggering DSO acceptance cycle (<2 weeks) | 8 | 3 | 13 | time | false | P2 |
| 14 | EXECUTE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of PCS-to-MV-transformer phase-rotation / protection-coordination errors causing repeat energization attempts | 8 | 5 | 11 | reliability | false | P2 |
| 15 | MONITOR | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of an undetected schedule-slippage signal propagating beyond 2 weeks before the IPP sponsor is notified | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | false | P1-Use |
| 16 | MONITOR | CONSISTENCY | Minimize the variability between EPC status reports and independent owner's-engineer findings (<10% KPI divergence) | 7 | 5 | 9 | reliability | false | P4 (influencer gaze) |
| 17 | MODIFY | EFFORT | Minimize the effort required to process a DSO-requested grid-code parameter change (<2 weeks from notice to implementation) | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | false | P2 |
| 18 | MODIFY | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of a change-order exceeding 3% of contract value through an opaque pricing mechanism | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | true (A2-caused lock-in) | P1-Acq |
| 19 | CONCLUDE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood that the as-commissioned AC-AC round-trip efficiency falls short of guaranteed value by >1.5 pp in Year 1 | 9 | 5 | 13 | cost | false | P2 |
| 20 | CONCLUDE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of Year-1 availability shortfall below 97% triggering liquidated-damages clauses | 9 | 5 | 13 | reliability | false | P2 |
| 21 | CONCLUDE | EFFORT | Minimize the number of separate warranty counterparties the IPP sponsor has to chase when a defect crosses battery / PCS / EMS domains (target: 1) | 8 | 3 | 13 | time | true (A4-caused) | P1-Maint |
| 22 | CONCLUDE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood that performance-guarantee telemetry from EMS / SCADA cannot be reconciled with SLA evidence at annual audit | 7 | 4 | 10 | reliability | false | P2 |

#### P3–P5 Bridge Needs (extracted via bridge questions)

| # | Level | Retrieval | Bridge → need |
|---|---|---|---|
| P3-a | Role (IPP sponsor) | "Being the operator who brought a large asset online on time" | Maximize the visibility of milestone completion to lender / board during construction — Imp 7, Sat 5, Opp 9, image |
| P4-a | Image (Owner's Engineer gaze) | Lender's TA must see EPC as competent | Maximize the transparency of interface-risk ownership in the EPC contract documentation — Imp 8, Sat 4, Opp 12, reliability |
| P5-a | Emotion (sponsor) | Avoid "rogue-asset anxiety" (fire, blackout, reputational) | Minimize the likelihood of any Feuerwehr-attended incident occurring in the Year-1 operational window [hypothesis] — Imp 10, Sat 6, Opp 14, safety |

### 3.4 ODI Scoring Summary

| Classification | Count | Needs |
|---|---|---|
| **Underserved** (Imp >7 AND Sat <5) | 11 | #1, #3, #4, #8, #11, #12, #13, #15, #18, #20, #21 (and P4-a, P5-a) |
| Mid-field | 11 | Rest |
| Overserved | 0 | — |
| **product_related=true** | 4 | #3, #7, #18, #21 (all arise from split-contract or vendor-lock alternatives) |
| product_related=false | 18 | Market-inherent |
| Minimize / Maximize mix | 19 / 3 | ~86 / 14 (skews minimize; needs review — Burleson expects ~60/40) |
| Relevant steps | 8 / 8 | All Ulwick steps active |
| Needs per step | 2–4 | ≥2 per step ✅ |

**Ratio check:** Market-inherent 82% / Tech-specific 18% — **outside 60–70 / 30–40 target**. Acceptable deviation: EPC-service needs are disproportionately market-inherent because the integration problems (grid code, DSO, fire code, permits) are exogenous. The four tech-specific needs correctly map to the *alternatives' trade-offs* (split-contract interfaces, OEM lock-in).

---

sections.title: Neo4j Write Preview
sections.content:
```
(Market {naics:"221118"}) -[:has_job_map]-> (JobMap {product_job_statement})
(JobMap) -[:anchored_at]-> (ValueNetworkUnit {name:"Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services", level:"L5"})
(JobMap) -[:has_job_step]-> (JobStep) x8 (Define..Conclude)
(JobStep) -[:has_error_statement]-> (ErrorStatement) x22
(Market) -[:has_need]-> (ErrorStatement) x22 (+3 P3-P5)
(Market) -[:has_incumbent_anchor]-> (IncumbentAnchor {technology:"OEM-led turnkey (Fluence/Tesla/Sungrow)"})
(IncumbentAnchor) -[:anchored_at]-> (ValueNetworkUnit {name:"Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services"})
```

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | All statements start with "Minimize the likelihood of…" / "Maximize…" + single KPI | ✅ |
| V2 | product_related=true only if need disappears with different alternative | ✅ (4/22 map to A2/A4 trade-offs) |
| V3 | Imp/Sat sanity — safety-critical at 10 | ✅ (#4, P5-a at 10) |
| V4 | Impact-category match KPI | ✅ |
| V5 | ≥2 per relevant step · duplicates zero | ✅ (2–4 per step; 22 across 8) |
| V6 | Solution-agnostic wording (no "lithium", "LFP", "Fluence" in statements) | ✅ |
| V7 | Anchor level matches abstraction (L5 for service-system) | ✅ |
| V8 | Product job names parent VN unit | ✅ ("Construction & Integration") |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [Success strategies for BESS investments — Watson Farley & Williams](https://www.wfw.com/articles/success-strategies-for-bess-investments-with-regard-to-regulation-and-construction/)
- [German large-scale BESS — FCA, grid & revenue challenges (Energy-Storage.News)](https://www.energy-storage.news/german-large-scale-bess-projects-have-built-industry-confidence-but-fca-grid-and-revenue-challenges-remain/)
- [German TSO grid connection requests 226 GW at year-turn (ESS News)](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/01/14/german-grid-companies-had-connection-requests-for-226-gw-of-big-batteries-at-turn-of-year/)
- [Global grid-scale BESS deployment +38% YoY through Oct 2025 (ESS News)](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/11/24/global-grid-scale-bess-deployment-up-by-38-year-on-year-through-october/)
- [Making BESS warranties work — contracts vs. reality (ESS News)](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/09/19/making-bess-warranties-work-contracts-vs-reality/)
- [EPC Framework for BESS Projects (arXiv)](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.13281v1)
- [Round-Trip Efficiency as a performance guarantee (Merus Power)](https://meruspower.com/blog/round-trip-efficiency-as-a-performance-guarantee-for-battery-energy-storage-systems/)
- [Germany to launch Inertia Service market 2026 — grid-forming BESS (HSF Kramer)](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/energy/2025-posts/germany-to-launch-inertia-service-market-in-2026-new-revenue-stream-for-grid-forming-bess)
- [VDE-AR-N 4110 TCR Medium Voltage](https://www.vde.com/en/fnn/topics/technical-connection-rules/tcr-for-medium-voltage)

rows.Value: Deliver dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy on demand to the power system, earning revenue from flexibility, capacity, and ancillary-service markets
Standalone BESS · PV+BESS hybrid · Wind+BESS hybrid · DSO Netzbooster · C&I behind-the-meter · HPC buffer
L7 ecosystem (IPP sells dispatched electricity + grid services)

rows.Circumstance: Fund- or developer-backed, greenfield projects, merchant or tolled revenue
RWE, EnBW, Vattenfall subsidiaries; procurement-heavy, regulated risk appetite
Brownfield retrofit onto operational renewables park; hybrid revenue
Passive equity; outsources everything to owner's engineer + EPC
Small-scale (5–50 MWh), local grid-service focus, public procurement
Entrix / Next Kraftwerke anchor tolling, finance via SPV

rows.Value: **PROCESS_VN** — EPC service creates the asset; does not become part of a consumable output
Product exists to serve L6d's need: **convert a greenlit storage business case into a commissioned, grid-compliant, revenue-generating asset**
"What integration-and-commissioning execution does an IPP need when converting a storage investment thesis into a running asset?"
"Turn my greenlit storage business case into a safe, certified, revenue-generating asset on schedule, on budget, and without me having to integrate it myself"

"Help Construction & Integration to deliver a commissioned, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8-certified multi-MWh BESS asset within contracted COD, CAPEX, and performance guarantees under single-source accountability"
PASS — ≥3 alternatives from different categories (OEM turnkey, pure-play EPC, split-contract, defer)

rows.Alternative: **Solar-EPC extension into BESS** (Mein Solar's category: BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Strong grid-code / civil depth, weaker cell & EMS OEM leverage; thin BESS reference count vs. OEM giants

rows.Alternative: **OEM-led turnkey** (Fluence, Tesla Megapack, Sungrow PowerTitan, Wärtsilä)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Equipment-bundled, deep cell/PCS integration; vendor lock-in, weaker local civil + permit execution, less PV-hybrid flexibility

rows.Alternative: **Pure-play BESS EPC integrator** (Kyon Energy, GreenTech Energy, SolarEast EPC)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Storage-first pedigree, OEM-agnostic procurement; smaller balance sheet, no PV-hybrid history
Lowest price ceiling, maximum OEM choice; owner carries all interface + schedule risk

rows.Alternative: **Defer or cancel project** (wait for cells to drop, policy clarity)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Zero CAPEX + zero integration burden; forgoes revenue window + permits + grid-capacity slot (226 GW DE queue)

rows.Notes: Mein Solar's size class — reference fit

rows.Scenario: PV-park retrofit (20–40 MWh BESS on operational PV)

rows.Notes: Above Mein Solar's 9 MWh reference; syndication required

rows.Instance: O&M provider (post-COD), DSO interface engineer, insurance underwriter
Owner's Engineer (DNV, Fichtner), Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier

rows.Rationale: EPC fixes scope, grid-code target, revenue-stack enablement, performance SLA
EPC runs grid-capacity study + Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung + equipment sourcing
EPC executes permitting, civil works, protection study, site logistics
FAT, SAT, protection-relay setting, trip tests, pre-cert readiness
Container placement, MV/LV install, energization, grid synchronization
Schedule, budget, HSE, Brandschutz, quality during the build
Change orders, grid-code adjustments, DSO-acceptance rework
FGW TR8 cert, DSO acceptance, handover dossier, performance guarantee bond

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of CAPEX-vs-actual deviation exceeding ±5% between EPC offer and final commissioned cost
Minimize the effort for the IPP sponsor to translate a business-case spreadsheet into a buildable, grid-code-compliant technical specification (<4 weeks)
Minimize the likelihood that more than 1 interface (battery / PCS / MV / EMS) carries ambiguous responsibility between EPC and supplier scopes
Minimize the likelihood of an undetected DSO capacity shortfall surfacing after FID (reducing delivered capacity >20%)
Maximize the rate of equipment-lead-time parallelization so that critical-path procurement (cells, PCS, transformer) stays <10 months
Minimize the likelihood of a single-vendor cell-supply concentration exposing >70% of project CAPEX to one country-of-origin tariff / CRMA risk
Minimize the number of separate Baugenehmigung, Brandschutzkonzept, and BImSchG submissions the IPP sponsor has to track (≤1 via EPC wrapper)
Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection design rework (additional walls, separation, hydrant runs) after local Feuerwehr review
Minimize the likelihood of civil rework (foundation, trenching) caused by late geotechnical surprises exceeding 5% of civil budget
Minimize the likelihood of a FAT/SAT protection-relay setting failing first trip test on energization day
Minimize the likelihood of an undetected unit-certificate (FGW TR3) documentation gap delaying plant-cert (TR8) submission >4 weeks
Maximize the likelihood of grid-synchronization within the contracted COD window (slip ≤30 days)
Minimize the time to recover from a failed commissioning test without re-triggering DSO acceptance cycle (<2 weeks)
Minimize the likelihood of PCS-to-MV-transformer phase-rotation / protection-coordination errors causing repeat energization attempts
Minimize the likelihood of an undetected schedule-slippage signal propagating beyond 2 weeks before the IPP sponsor is notified
Minimize the variability between EPC status reports and independent owner's-engineer findings (<10% KPI divergence)
Minimize the effort required to process a DSO-requested grid-code parameter change (<2 weeks from notice to implementation)
Minimize the likelihood of a change-order exceeding 3% of contract value through an opaque pricing mechanism
Minimize the likelihood that the as-commissioned AC-AC round-trip efficiency falls short of guaranteed value by >1.5 pp in Year 1
Minimize the likelihood of Year-1 availability shortfall below 97% triggering liquidated-damages clauses
Minimize the number of separate warranty counterparties the IPP sponsor has to chase when a defect crosses battery / PCS / EMS domains (target: 1)
Minimize the likelihood that performance-guarantee telemetry from EMS / SCADA cannot be reconciled with SLA evidence at annual audit

rows.Retrieval: "Being the operator who brought a large asset online on time"
rows.Bridge → need: Maximize the visibility of milestone completion to lender / board during construction — Imp 7, Sat 5, Opp 9, image

rows.Retrieval: Lender's TA must see EPC as competent
rows.Bridge → need: Maximize the transparency of interface-risk ownership in the EPC contract documentation — Imp 8, Sat 4, Opp 12, reliability
Minimize the likelihood of any Feuerwehr-attended incident occurring in the Year-1 operational window [hypothesis] — Imp 10, Sat 6, Opp 14, safety

rows.Needs: #3, #7, #18, #21 (all arise from split-contract or vendor-lock alternatives)

rows.Check: All statements start with "Minimize the likelihood of…" / "Maximize…" + single KPI
product_related=true only if need disappears with different alternative
Anchor level matches abstraction (L5 for service-system)
Product job names parent VN unit

entities.cfj: Deliver dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy on demand to the power system, earning revenue from flexibility, capacity, and ancillary-service markets
entities.core_job: Turn my greenlit storage business case into a safe, certified, revenue-generating asset on schedule, on budget, and without me having to integrate it myself
entities.product_job_statement: Help Construction & Integration to deliver a commissioned, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8-certified multi-MWh BESS asset within contracted COD, CAPEX, and performance guarantees under single-source accountability

segments.name: Pure-play BESS IPP
segments.circumstance: Fund- or developer-backed, merchant or tolled revenue

segments.name: Municipal Stadtwerke / coop
segments.circumstance: 5–50 MWh, local grid service, public procurement

alternatives.name: Solar-EPC extension into BESS (Mein Solar, BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Grid-code & civil depth; thin BESS reference count vs. OEM giants

alternatives.name: OEM-led turnkey (Fluence, Tesla Megapack, Sungrow, Wärtsilä)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Deep cell/PCS integration; vendor lock-in; weaker local civil + permit; less PV-hybrid flexibility

alternatives.name: Pure-play BESS EPC integrator (Kyon, GreenTech, SolarEast EPC)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Storage-first pedigree, OEM-agnostic; smaller balance sheet; no PV-hybrid history

alternatives.name: Split-contract / owner-managed multi-prime (DNV/Fichtner PMC + separate lots)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Lowest price ceiling; owner owns interface & schedule risk

alternatives.name: Defer or cancel project
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero CAPEX, zero burden; forgoes revenue window, grid-queue slot (226 GW DE backlog)

stakeholders.instance: O&M provider, DSO interface engineer, insurance underwriter
Owner's Engineer (DNV, Fichtner), Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier

job_steps.relevance_rationale: EPC fixes scope, grid-code target, revenue-stack enablement, SLA
EPC runs grid-capacity study + Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung + equipment sourcing
EPC executes permitting, civil, protection study
FAT, SAT, protection-relay setting, pre-cert readiness
Container placement, MV/LV install, grid synchronization
Schedule, budget, HSE oversight during build
FGW TR8 cert, handover, performance guarantee bond

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of CAPEX-vs-actual deviation exceeding ±5% between EPC offer and final commissioned cost
error_statements.rationale: DE BESS schedule/cost risk is material given 226 GW grid queue, DSO FCA rules, supply chain FX
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbents (Fluence/Tesla/Sungrow) offer fixed-price turnkey but embed margin buffers and change-order opacity that still leave IPP sponsors exposed to post-FID cost drift, keeping satisfaction moderate at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the effort for the IPP sponsor to translate a business-case spreadsheet into a buildable, grid-code-compliant technical specification (<4 weeks)
error_statements.rationale: Many sponsors outsource this to owner's engineer at cost; single-source EPC can fold into offer phase
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM-led turnkey packages bundle spec translation into the sales cycle, but IPP sponsors with bespoke grid-code or revenue-stacking requirements still need separate owner's-engineer input, yielding moderate satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that more than 1 interface (battery / PCS / MV / EMS) carries ambiguous responsibility between EPC and supplier scopes
error_statements.rationale: Interface gaps are the #1 claim driver in split-contract delivery
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent OEM-turnkey wraps as single counterparty but equipment-locked — partial mitigation.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of an undetected DSO capacity shortfall surfacing after FID (reducing delivered capacity >20%)
error_statements.rationale: German TSO queue 226 GW; many connection agreements later curtailed under Flexible Connection Agreements
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: DSO capacity shortfalls are a systemic German grid-queue issue (226 GW backlog) that no incumbent EPC fully mitigates; early-stage grid studies remain sponsor-driven, leaving satisfaction low at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Maximize the rate of equipment-lead-time parallelization so that critical-path procurement (cells, PCS, transformer) stays <10 months
error_statements.rationale: MV transformer lead time in DE peaked at 24+ months 2023-2025; parallelization is the main lever
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM vertically integrated supply chains (Tesla Megafactory, Sungrow in-house PCS) shorten some lead times, but MV transformer and BoP procurement remain market-constrained at 18-24 months, capping satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of a single-vendor cell-supply concentration exposing >70% of project CAPEX to one country-of-origin tariff / CRMA risk
error_statements.rationale: CRMA / EU Battery Reg. and CN-tariff exposure top of sponsor diligence in 2026
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: No incumbent OEM meaningfully diversifies LFP cell sourcing away from Chinese suppliers; CRMA and tariff exposure remains unaddressed across the market, holding satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the number of separate Baugenehmigung, Brandschutzkonzept, and BImSchG submissions the IPP sponsor has to track (≤1 via EPC wrapper)
error_statements.rationale: Split-contract forces sponsor to coordinate permits directly
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM turnkey wraps consolidate some permit responsibility but still delegate Baugenehmigung and BImSchG to local subcontractors; the sponsor coordination burden is reduced but not eliminated, yielding satisfaction of 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection design rework (additional walls, separation, hydrant runs) after local Feuerwehr review
error_statements.rationale: Feuerwehr guidance varies by Bundesland; rework common on first-of-kind site
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: No incumbent profile has DE Feuerwehr-specific edge — hold.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of civil rework (foundation, trenching) caused by late geotechnical surprises exceeding 5% of civil budget
error_statements.rationale: Managed via early site survey; mature industry practice
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Geotechnical risk management is mature standard practice in DE infrastructure; most EPC contractors include early site surveys, keeping civil rework rates low and satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of a FAT/SAT protection-relay setting failing first trip test on energization day
error_statements.rationale: First-trip failures add 2-4 weeks + DSO re-attendance fees
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: FAT/SAT relay-setting failures are procedural rather than systemic; incumbent OEMs with factory-tested PCS integration reduce but do not eliminate first-trip risk, leaving satisfaction moderate at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of an undetected unit-certificate (FGW TR3) documentation gap delaying plant-cert (TR8) submission >4 weeks
error_statements.rationale: FGW TR3 gaps are the #1 cert-delay cause; requires proactive dossier discipline
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Fluence uses local subcontractor for DE cert; OEM incumbents have no DE-cert edge — hold.

error_statements.full_statement: Maximize the likelihood of grid-synchronization within the contracted COD window (slip ≤30 days)
error_statements.rationale: COD slip triggers lender-DSCR covenants, lost revenue window, LD clauses
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: COD-window adherence depends on DSO scheduling, grid-queue position, and permit timelines largely outside EPC control; incumbent track records are mixed, keeping satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the time to recover from a failed commissioning test without re-triggering DSO acceptance cycle (<2 weeks)
error_statements.rationale: Re-triggered DSO cycle can cost 6-10 weeks
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: No incumbent profile addresses DSO-test-failure recovery specifically — hold.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of PCS-to-MV-transformer phase-rotation / protection-coordination errors causing repeat energization attempts
error_statements.rationale: Common error mode on first-of-site builds; procedural fix
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Phase-rotation and protection-coordination errors are well-understood failure modes with established commissioning protocols; incumbent PCS-transformer packages reduce incidence, supporting a satisfaction of 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of an undetected schedule-slippage signal propagating beyond 2 weeks before the IPP sponsor is notified
error_statements.rationale: Late notification prevents sponsor mitigation; opacity in traditional EPC reporting
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Schedule transparency varies widely across incumbent EPC providers; OEM-turnkey reporting is often opaque on subcontractor milestones, leaving IPP sponsors with limited early-warning capability at 4/10 satisfaction.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the variability between EPC status reports and independent owner's-engineer findings (<10% KPI divergence)
error_statements.rationale: Divergence erodes lender confidence and triggers extra audit cycles
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM status reporting and independent owner's-engineer findings diverge primarily on BMS and thermal metrics; standardized KPI frameworks are emerging but not yet universal, yielding moderate satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the effort required to process a DSO-requested grid-code parameter change (<2 weeks from notice to implementation)
error_statements.rationale: DSO parameter changes (FCA-era) now routine; slow response extends commissioning
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: DSO grid-code parameter changes under FCA-era rules are becoming routine in DE; most EPC contractors can process changes but timelines vary by vendor responsiveness, leaving satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of a change-order exceeding 3% of contract value through an opaque pricing mechanism
error_statements.rationale: OEM-led turnkey (A2) frequently price-locks the owner via equipment dependency
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM incumbent weakness; incumbent sat lowered.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that the as-commissioned AC-AC round-trip efficiency falls short of guaranteed value by >1.5 pp in Year 1
error_statements.rationale: Each 1 pp RTE = ~0.37% revenue; LD typically 2–4× kWh-shortfall damages
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM native PCS-BMS integration tighter — raised.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of Year-1 availability shortfall below 97% triggering liquidated-damages clauses
error_statements.rationale: Availability below 97-98% breaches standard LD threshold; cost scales with downtime hours
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Year-1 availability above 97% is achievable with OEM-integrated BMS and thermal management, but first-of-kind sites and commissioning-phase teething issues still create LD exposure, keeping satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the number of separate warranty counterparties the IPP sponsor has to chase when a defect crosses battery / PCS / EMS domains (target: 1)
error_statements.rationale: Split-contract (A4) forces sponsor into cross-vendor dispute; single-source EPC wraps this
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM turnkey mitigates partially (single counterparty) — raised.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that performance-guarantee telemetry from EMS / SCADA cannot be reconciled with SLA evidence at annual audit
error_statements.rationale: SLA-audit gaps are the emerging friction point as BESS SLAs mature
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM-native BMS telemetry better than arm's-length integrator — raised.

p3_p5_needs.retrieval: Being the operator who brought a large asset online on time
p3_p5_needs.bridge_need: Maximize the visibility of milestone completion to lender / board during construction

p3_p5_needs.retrieval: Lender's TA must see EPC as competent
p3_p5_needs.bridge_need: Maximize the transparency of interface-risk ownership in the EPC contract documentation
Minimize the likelihood of any Feuerwehr-attended incident occurring in the Year-1 operational window

incumbent_anchor.technology_name: OEM-led turnkey (Fluence / Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan / Wärtsilä)
incumbent_anchor.rationale: Currently dominant alternative (~40% share in DE) against which Mein Solar's solar-EPC-extension positioning is evaluated

coverage_check.ratio_note: Outside target 60/40 — EPC-service needs are disproportionately market-inherent because integration problems are exogenous (grid code, DSO, fire)
